author: Ahmad Khan, Sir Sayad
title: Conscience, Zamir
gloss: A lecture on Conscience from the Muhammadan point of view.
notes: Sir Syed reprint series continues (Vakil Trading Co.).

author: Ahmad Khan, Sir Sayad, and Charagh Ali, Maulvi
title: Haqiqat-us-Sihr
gloss: Lectures on the Islamic conception of necromancy.
notes: Joint reprint: Sir Syed (dash-continuation) and Charagh Ali on magic/necromancy — rationalist theology.

author: Ahmad Khan, Sir Sayad
title: Mahdi-i-Akhir-uz-Zaman
gloss: A booklet in which the writer tries to prove that the belief of the Muhammadans that the Mahdi, the twelfth Imam, will re-appear towards the end of this world, is based on unreliable traditions.
notes: Dash-continuation follows the joint entry at serial 57; attributed to Sir Sayad Ahmad Khan (his known anti-Mahdist essay).

flags.issue: dash-continuation after joint authorship — could include Charagh Ali

title: Aina-i-Qadiyani
gloss: A pamphlet directed against Mirza Ghulam Ahmad of Qádián.
notes: Anti-Ahmadi polemic — printed alongside Ahmadi works this same quarter (p41 s39).

flags.issue: printed '1,009' — possibly misprint for 1,000; transcribed as printed

author: Charagh Ali, Maulvi
title: Hasrat-i-Isa aur Salib
gloss: A booklet in which the writer, who is a follower of Mirza Ghulam Ahmad of Qádián, tries to prove, with quotations from the Quran and the traditions, that Christ was not crucified but died a natural death and that he does not live, and will return to this world as believed by the Muhammadans and Christians.
notes: Ahmadi christology (natural death of Jesus). Catalogue's gloss reads 'and will return' where 'will not return' is evidently intended — transcribed as printed.

author: Charagh Ali, Maulvi
title: Ihsán-i-'Am
gloss: A small pamphlet in which the writer tries to explain, with quotations from the Quran, that Islam enjoins kindness to all mankind.

author: Gokal Das, Mahant
title: Swami Daya Nand ji ki das fash ghalatian
gloss: A tract in which the writer points out ten gross mistakes committed by Swami Daya Nand, the founder of the Arya Samaj, in making quotations from the Vedas, etc.
notes: Sanatanist anti-Arya Samaj polemic; same Mahant Gokal Das as p41 s40.

title: Kalid-ut-Tauhid
gloss: A booklet on sufi-ism, containing instructions for disciples, trnnslated by Maulvi Abdus Sattar of Tonk from the original by Hazrat Bahu, a well-kuown Muhammadan saint of the Punjab.
publisher: Malak Fazl-ud-din Chanan Din and Taj-ud-din, Book-sellers
notes: Sultan Bahu's Kalid-ut-Tauhid in Urdu translation. [Previous edition noticed in entry No. 232 at page 39 of the Catalogue for the quarter ending the 31st March, 1906.]
